title: Schedule of Cash Collections
event: Reyes Co. collects 60% of sales in the month of sale and 40% in the following month. Sales were $100,000 in May and $120,000 in June. Compute June cash collections.

computationPaths.narration: Each month's cash is a blend: this month's fast collections plus last month's stragglers.

memorize.body: Month's collections = current sales × current-month % + prior sales × following-month %.
Collections ≠ sales. June SOLD 120,000 but COLLECTS 112,000 — the timing lag is the entire lesson.

questions.prompt: 60% collected in month of sale, 40% next month. May sales $100,000, June sales $120,000. JUNE collections = ?

distractors.feedback: That's June's SALES. Collections mix 60% of June with 40% of May: 112,000.
Percentages crossed (40% of June + 60% of May). The month of sale gets the 60%: 72,000 + 40,000.
